content.subtitle: Use
content.description: Certain verbs only require a subject to form a complete sentence. No object is needed.

content.subtitle: Use
content.description: A large number of verbs take a single object after them to complete their meaning.

content.subtitle: Use
content.description: Certain verbs can take two objects at the same time. One is the direct object (the thing) and the other is the indirect object (usually a person).

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: Two prepositions are commonly placed between the direct object and the indirect object: **to** and **for**.
items: She bought a gift **for** her brother.

content.description: These verbs can also be used in the passive.
items: A promotion was offered to Tom.

content.subtitle: Be careful
items: When the indirect object is placed before the direct object, do not add a preposition.
Correct: She gave Leo the keys.
Incorrect: She gave to Leo the keys.

sections.title: A: Complete with the correct form
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with one suitable verb.

items.sentence: After dinner, Grandpa ...... in his chair.
I ...... my backpack next to the door.
Can you ...... me your phone charger?
Jake ...... the door quietly when he left.
Everyone ...... at Sarah's funny story.
Mum ...... me a text message this morning.

sections.title: B: Choose the correct sentence
sections.instructions: Choose the sentence that is grammatically correct.

items.sentence: A Emma gave Jack a postcard. / B Emma gave to Jack a postcard.
A I bought my cousin some headphones. / B I bought for my cousin some headphones.
A Dad showed us his holiday photos. / B Dad showed to us his holiday photos.
A They sent us a package yesterday. / B They sent to us a package yesterday.
A She read the email to her manager. / B She read to her manager the email.
A Oliver wrote his friend a postcard. / B Oliver wrote to his friend a postcard.

sections.title: C: Choose the correct sentence
sections.instructions: Choose the sentence that uses to or for correctly. Click A or B.

items.sentenceA: Ella gave the keys to her brother.
items.sentenceB: Ella gave the keys for her brother.

items.sentenceA: My aunt bought a jacket to me.
items.sentenceB: My aunt bought a jacket for me.

items.sentenceA: The coach explained the rules to us.
items.sentenceB: The coach explained the rules for us.

items.sentenceA: Ben made dinner to his parents.
items.sentenceB: Ben made dinner for his parents.

items.sentenceA: I wrote an email for my teacher.
items.sentenceB: I wrote an email to my teacher.

items.sentenceA: The company offered a discount to customers.
items.sentenceB: The company offered a discount for customers.

sections.title: D: Gap fill (direct and indirect objects)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Could you pass ...... the bread, please?
Can you explain the homework ...... me again?
My father taught ...... how to swim.
We paid ...... cashier before leaving the shop.
She sent an invitation ...... her grandparents.
Could you make a sandwich ...... me?

sections.title: E: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake. Write the correct sentence.

items.sentence: Lucy gave to Ben her old laptop.
items.answer: Lucy gave Ben her old laptop.

items.sentence: Dad bought to me a bicycle.
Please show me to the manager.
items.answer: Please show the manager to me.

items.sentence: They offered to him a free ticket.
items.answer: They offered him a free ticket.

items.sentence: I wrote to my cousin a long email.
items.answer: I wrote my cousin a long email.

items.sentence: The teacher read to the students the story.
items.answer: The teacher read the students the story.

sections.title: F: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning.

items.sentence:
I bought my dad a magazine. **FOR**
I bought a magazine ...... dad.

She sent a message to Amy. **SENT**
She ...... message.

Could you give this book to me? **ME**
Could you ...... book?

He cooked dinner for us. **COOKED**
He ...... dinner.

The guide showed the paintings to the visitors. **SHOWED**
The guide ...... paintings.

Don't send your address to strangers. **SEND**
Don't ...... address.
